q: The planet Earth is the home to all living and non-living things that are ___ and interdependent on each other to thrive.
The environment contributes greatly to the growth of ___ and other organisms.
Challenges of environmental issues, such as ___ and waste materials from industrial and nuclear energy sectors, must be well informed to the society.
The environment is the surroundings comprising of hydrosphere, lithosphere, ___ and biosphere.
Both natural and man-made chemical substances in the environment have a direct impact on human ___ and development.
In the natural matter cycling, carbon, nitrogen, oxygen, phosphorus, and ___ take a variety of chemical forms.
The pathways that allow different chemical substances to move through living and non-living forms and their locations in the biosphere are called a ___ cycle.
The cycling of elements is interconnected with the ___ cycle.
Mineral nutrients are cycled, either rapidly or slowly, through the entire ___ and from one living organism to another.
Most of the Earth’s carbon is stored in ___ and sediments.
The carbon cycle is the biogeochemical cycle by which carbon is exchanged among the biosphere, geosphere, hydrosphere, and ___ of the Earth.
Plants release ___ when they decay.
___ is the process of gas exchange between the atmosphere and living organisms.
In the fossilisation process, the animal and plant residues become sediment and eventually turn into ___ and minerals.
The combustion of fossil fuels and organic materials gives off carbon dioxide, ___, and energy.
The nitrogen cycle involves the following steps: nitrogen fixation, nitrification, nitrogen ___, ammonification, and denitrification.
Phosphorus is an essential nutrient for plants and animals in the form of ions $PO_4^{3-}$ and $HPO_4^{2-}$.
Phosphate salts that are released from rocks through ___ usually dissolve in water present in soil and will be absorbed by plants.
Marine birds play a unique role in the phosphorus cycle, as they take phosphorus-containing fish out of the ocean and return to land, where they ___.
___ is one of the components that make up proteins and vitamins.
When sulphur dioxide enters the atmosphere, it will react with oxygen to produce sulphur trioxide gas ($SO_3$).
Sulphuric acid may also be produced from ___ emitted to the atmosphere by plankton species.
The ocean absorbs much of the carbon dioxide that is released from burning ___.
Extra carbon dioxide is lowering the ocean’s pH, through a process called ___.
The 7 R's of a green environment for sustainable development are Rethink, Refuse, Reduce, Reuse, Repair, Regift, and ___.
